questions.original_question: Did Zeekr undergo any change of holding structure?
questions.answer:
As of the latest information available, Zeekr underwent a significant restructuring in 2021 to establish ZEEKR Intelligent Technology Holding Limited as its overseas holding company. This reorganization was part of a broader strategy to create an independent Battery Electric Vehicles (BEV) business, attract external investment, and prepare for an overseas listing. The restructuring involved transferring various BEV-related businesses from Geely Holding and Geely Auto to ZEEKR Intelligent Technology Holding Limited. Since then, there have been no further changes reported in Zeekr's holding structure.

The information is based on knowledge available before 2024-12-31.

rag_info.chunk_content: Lotus Technology began as a business unit within Geely Auto in October 2017. Under the leadership of the co-founders, Mr. Shufu Li, Mr. Conghui An, Mr. Donghui Li, and Mr. Shengyue Gui, Lotus Technology incorporated ZEEKR Intelligent Technology as an exempted company with limited liability in March 2021 under the law of the Cayman Islands to act as its holding company. In April 2021, Zeekr Innovation, currently a wholly-owned subsidiary of ZEEKR Intelligent Technology, was incorporated under the laws of the British Virgin Islands. In the same period, Zeekr Technology, currently a wholly-owned subsidiary of Zeekr Innovation, was incorporated under the laws of Hong Kong. In April 2021, Lotus Technology announced the launch of its first BEV model, Zeekr 001, and started delivery from October 2021. Subsequently, Lotus Technology commenced deliveries of various upgraded models, including but not limited to Zeekr 001 FR in October 2023 and upgraded Zeekr 001 in February 2024. In July 2021, ZEEKR Shanghai acquired 100% equity interest in ZEEKR Hangzhou Bay from Geely Holding. In July 2021, Zhejiang ZEEKR was incorporated in the People's Republic of China and is currently a wholly-owned subsidiary of Zeekr Innovation. In August 2021, ZEEKR Innovation acquired 100% equity interest in ZEEKR Shanghai (99% from Geely Auto and 1% from Geely Holding). In October 2021, Lotus Technology acquired a 51% equity interest in Ningbo Viridi, which was previously wholly-owned by Geely Holding. In February 2022, Lotus Technology acquired 100% equity interest in Zeekr Tech EU from Geely Holding.

rag_info.chunk_content: ZEEKR Intelligent Technology Holding Limited (the “Company” or “Zeekr”) was incorporated under the laws of the Cayman Islands on March 31, 2021. The Company, together with its subsidiaries (collectively, the “Group”), is principally engaged in the commercialization and sales of electric vehicles and batteries, as well as the provision of automotive-related research and development services. Prior to the establishment of ZEEKR Intelligent Technology Holding Limited, the Group’s businesses were conducted through the following entities: (i) ZEEKR Automobile (Shanghai) Co., Ltd., previously known as Shanghai Maple Guorun Automobile Company Ltd. (“Zeekr Shanghai”); (ii) ZEEKR Automobile (Ningbo Hangzhou Bay New Zone) Co., Ltd. (“Zeekr Hangzhou Bay”); (iii) Viridi E-Mobility Technology (Ningbo) Co., Ltd. (“Ningbo Viridi”); (iv) ZEEKR Technology Europe AB (“ZTE”, previously known as China-Euro Vehicle Technology Aktiebolag or “CEVT”). In 2021, in an effort to establish an independent Battery Electric Vehicles (BEV) business to obtain investment from outside investors and in preparation for an overseas listing, Zhejiang Geely Holding Group Co., Ltd. (“Geely Holding”), the controlling shareholder of Geely Automobile Holdings Limited (“Geely Auto”), undertook a series of contemplated reorganizations (the “Reorganization”). The main purpose of the Reorganization was to establish ZEEKR Intelligent Technology Holding Limited as the overseas holding company for the above BEV-related businesses that were originally operated within Geely Holding and Geely Auto. Subsequent to the establishment of ZEEKR Intelligent Technology Holding Limited in March 2021, the above businesses were transferred to ZEEKR Intelligent Technology Holding Limited by Geely Holding and Geely Auto as part of the Reorganization.

rag_info.chunk_content: As these entities being transferred are all under the common control of Geely Holding, these transfers were accounted for as a restructuring of entities under common control as follows: In July 2021, ZEEKR Intelligent Technology Holding Limited established Zhejiang ZEEKR Intelligent Technology Co., Ltd (“Zhejiang Zeekr”) in the People’s Republic of China (“PRC”), a wholly-owned subsidiary. In July 2021, ZEEKR Automobile (Shanghai) Co., Ltd., a subsidiary of Geely Automobile Holdings Limited, acquired 100% equity interest in ZEEKR Automobile (Ningbo Hangzhou Bay New Zone) Co., Ltd. from Geely Holding for a cash consideration of RMB485.3 million. The net book value of ZEEKR Automobile (Ningbo Hangzhou Bay New Zone) Co., Ltd. at the time of acquisition was RMB500 million. The corresponding difference between acquisition consideration paid and the entity’s net book value of RMB14,671 was recognized in additional paid-in capital. In August 2021, the Group through Zhejiang Zeekr acquired 100% equity interest in ZEEKR Automobile (Shanghai) Co., Ltd. (99% from Geely Automobile Holdings Limited and 1% from Geely Holding) for a cash consideration of RMB980.4 million. The net book value of ZEEKR Automobile (Shanghai) Co., Ltd. was RMB970.4 million at the time of acquisition. The corresponding difference between acquisition consideration paid and the entity’s net book value of RMB10,032 loss was recognized in additional paid-in capital.

metadata.title_summary:
title: 1. Principal activities and history of the Group 
summary: ZEEKR Intelligent Technology Holding Limited, established on March 31, 2021, in the Cayman Islands, specializes in the commercialization and sales of electric vehicles and batteries, along with providing automotive-related research and development services through its subsidiaries. The company emerged from a reorganization initiated by Zhejiang Geely Holding Group Co., Ltd., which aimed to create an independent Battery Electric Vehicles (BEV) business to attract external investment and prepare for an overseas listing. This reorganization involved transferring various entities, including ZEEKR Automobile (Shanghai) Co., Ltd., ZEEKR Automobile (Ningbo Hangzhou Bay New Zone) Co., Ltd., Viridi E-Mobility Technology (Ningbo) Co., Ltd., and ZEEKR Technology Europe AB, under the common control of Geely Holding to Zeekr.

In July 2021, ZEEKR established a wholly-owned subsidiary in China and subsequently acquired full equity stakes in Zeekr Hangzhou Bay and Zeekr Shanghai, incurring minor losses in additional paid-in capital. Additionally, Zeekr Shanghai acquired a 51% interest in Ningbo Viridi and later, in February 2022, ZTE from Geely Holding, further solidifying its operational structure. By May 2024, ZEEKR achieved a significant milestone by successfully listing on the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE), marking its growth and establishment in the electric vehicle market.

title: 
summary: The financial overview of Zeekr Intelligent Technology Holding Limited, as detailed in the combined and consolidated statements for the fiscal years ending December 31, 2021, 2022, and 2023, reveals significant changes in shareholders' equity and overall financial performance. The company began 2021 with no issued shares and total equity of RMB 3,379,969. Throughout the years, it underwent substantial transactions, including a capital injection of RMB 500,000 and the issuance of 2 billion ordinary shares, which contributed to a total paid-in capital of RMB 4,269,555 by the end of 2021. However, the company faced challenges, including a cumulative comprehensive loss of RMB 4,584,927, resulting in a total equity of RMB 929,426.

By 2023, Zeekr reported 2 billion ordinary shares and approximately 265.8 million preferred shares, with additional paid-in capital rising to RMB 11.2 billion. Despite these increases, the accumulated deficit escalated to RMB (20.9 billion), reflecting a net loss of RMB (8.3 billion) for the year. The total shareholders' equity decreased to RMB (8.7 billion), indicating ongoing financial difficulties.

The cash flow statements highlighted the company's struggles, with net losses from operating activities growing from RMB 4.5 billion in 2021 to RMB 8.3 billion in 2023. Share-based compensation and depreciation expenses also rose, underscoring the financial strain. The adjustments in accounts receivable and inventory further illustrated the challenges faced by Zeekr in managing its financial health.

Overall, the combined financial statements provide a comprehensive view of Zeekr's equity structure, financial performance, and cash flow dynamics, emphasizing the impact of various transactions and losses on the company's fiscal standing.

Ultimately, the total shareholders' deficit for 2024 was about RMB 10.15 billion, reflecting the ongoing financial adjustments and challenges faced by the company.

This summary encapsulates the dynamic nature of ZEEKR's financial position, highlighting the impact of share issuances, deficits, and comprehensive income adjustments over the specified periods.

summary: ZEEKR Intelligent Technology Holding Limited, established in the Cayman Islands on March 31, 2021, focuses on the commercialization and sales of electric vehicles (EVs) and batteries, alongside providing automotive-related research and development services through its subsidiaries. The formation of ZEEKR was part of a strategic restructuring by Zhejiang Geely Holding Group Co., Ltd. to create an independent Battery Electric Vehicles (BEV) business, aimed at attracting external investment and preparing for an overseas listing. This restructuring involved transferring various entities previously managed under Geely Holding and Geely Auto into ZEEKR.

Key subsidiaries within the ZEEKR Group include ZEEKR Automobile (Shanghai) Co., Ltd., which serves as an investment holding company, and ZEEKR Automobile (Ningbo Hangzhou Bay New Zone) Co., Ltd., responsible for the production and commercialization of EVs. Additionally, Viridi E-Mobility Technology (Ningbo) Co., Ltd. specializes in electric powertrains and battery packs, while Zeekr Technology Europe AB, based in Sweden, focuses on research and development.

The restructuring process is highlighted by significant transactions, such as the acquisition of ZEEKR Hangzhou Bay and ZEEKR Shanghai, which involved adjustments in equity interests and additional paid-in capital. Overall, ZEEKR represents a consolidated effort to streamline and enhance the electric vehicle business under Geely's broader corporate strategy.

summary: ZEEKR Intelligent Technology Holding Limited, established in the Cayman Islands on March 31, 2021, is dedicated to the commercialization and sales of electric vehicles (EVs) and batteries, alongside providing automotive-related research and development services. The company emerged from a restructuring of operations previously managed by Zhejiang Geely Holding Group Co., Ltd., with the aim of creating a focused battery electric vehicle (BEV) business to attract investment and facilitate overseas listings.

The ZEEKR Group comprises several entities, each with specific roles in the EV ecosystem. Key entities include ZEEKR Automobile (Shanghai) Co., Ltd., an investment holding company; ZEEKR Automobile (Ningbo Hangzhou Bay New Zone) Co., Ltd., which handles EV production and commercialization; Viridi E-Mobility Technology (Ningbo) Co., Ltd., focused on electric powertrains and battery packs; and Zeekr Technology Europe AB in Sweden, specializing in research and development.

The restructuring process involved transferring relevant businesses to ZEEKR, which was recognized as a holding company for BEV operations previously under Geely Holding. In July 2021, ZEEKR established a wholly-owned subsidiary in China and acquired ZEEKR Hangzhou Bay from Geely Holding for RMB485.3 million, followed by the acquisition of ZEEKR Shanghai for RMB980.4 million in August. These transactions, treated as restructuring under common control, aimed to streamline operations and enhance ZEEKR's strategic focus on advancing electric vehicle commercialization and supporting related activities.

title: Corporate Structure 
summary: The corporate structure diagram of The Company, based in the Cayman Islands, provides a detailed overview of its ownership hierarchy and key subsidiaries as of the specified date. It highlights both direct and indirect subsidiaries across various regions, emphasizing ownership percentages that reflect full or partial control. Notably, The Company fully owns ZEEKR Technology Innovation Limited in the British Virgin Islands, which in turn completely owns ZEEKR Technology Limited in Hong Kong. This structure connects offshore entities to onshore operations, with ZEEKR Technology Limited having complete ownership of Zhejiang ZEEKR.

Zhejiang ZEEKR itself oversees several subsidiaries, including CEVT in Sweden, acquired in February 2022, where it holds a 100% equity interest. ZEEKR Automobile (Shanghai) Co., Ltd. also maintains full ownership of ZEEKR Automobile (Ningbo Hangzhou Bay New Zone) Co., Ltd., following an acquisition in July 2021. Additionally, ZEEKR Automobile (Shanghai) Co., Ltd. owns a 51% stake in Ningbo Viridi, which focuses on R&D and production of new energy vehicle (NEV) components, after acquiring this interest in October 2021. Other wholly-owned subsidiaries under Zhejiang ZEEKR include Zhejiang ZEEKR Automobile Research & Development Co., Ltd., Zhejiang ZEEKR Automobile Sales Co., Ltd., and Zhejiang ZEEKR Zhixing Technology Service Co., Ltd.

The diagram also addresses regulatory and business challenges related to auditing and inspections in mainland China and Hong Kong, along with specific acquisition details. CEVT, established in 2013 as an indirect subsidiary of Geely Holding, specializes in R&D for software systems and engineering related to NEVs. The acquisition of CEVT was finalized on February 1, 2022. Similarly, ZEEKR Automobile (Shanghai) Co., Ltd. completed its acquisition of ZEEKR Automobile (Ningbo Hangzhou Bay New Zone) Co., Ltd. in July 2021, while Ningbo Viridi, founded in 2017, became 51% owned by ZEEKR following its acquisition in October 2021. This subsidiary has been generating revenue since March 2019.

metadata.title_summary:
title: 1. Principal activities and history of the Group 
summary: ZEEKR Intelligent Technology Holding Limited, established on March 31, 2021, in the Cayman Islands, specializes in the commercialization and sales of electric vehicles and batteries, along with providing automotive-related research and development services through its subsidiaries. The company emerged from a reorganization initiated by Zhejiang Geely Holding Group Co., Ltd., which aimed to create an independent Battery Electric Vehicles (BEV) business to attract external investment and prepare for an overseas listing. This reorganization involved transferring various entities, including ZEEKR Automobile (Shanghai) Co., Ltd., ZEEKR Automobile (Ningbo Hangzhou Bay New Zone) Co., Ltd., Viridi E-Mobility Technology (Ningbo) Co., Ltd., and ZEEKR Technology Europe AB, under the common control of Geely Holding to Zeekr.

In July 2021, ZEEKR established a wholly-owned subsidiary in China and subsequently acquired full equity stakes in Zeekr Hangzhou Bay and Zeekr Shanghai, incurring minor losses in additional paid-in capital. Additionally, Zeekr Shanghai acquired a 51% interest in Ningbo Viridi and later, in February 2022, ZTE from Geely Holding, further solidifying its operational structure. By May 2024, ZEEKR achieved a significant milestone by successfully listing on the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE), marking its growth and establishment in the electric vehicle market.

metadata.title_summary:
title: 1. Principal activities and history of the group 
summary: ZEEKR Intelligent Technology Holding Limited, established in the Cayman Islands on March 31, 2021, is dedicated to the commercialization and sales of electric vehicles (EVs) and batteries, alongside providing automotive-related research and development services. The company emerged from a restructuring of operations previously managed by Zhejiang Geely Holding Group Co., Ltd., with the aim of creating a focused battery electric vehicle (BEV) business to attract investment and facilitate overseas listings.

The ZEEKR Group comprises several entities, each with specific roles in the EV ecosystem. Key entities include ZEEKR Automobile (Shanghai) Co., Ltd., an investment holding company; ZEEKR Automobile (Ningbo Hangzhou Bay New Zone) Co., Ltd., which handles EV production and commercialization; Viridi E-Mobility Technology (Ningbo) Co., Ltd., focused on electric powertrains and battery packs; and Zeekr Technology Europe AB in Sweden, specializing in research and development.

The restructuring process involved transferring relevant businesses to ZEEKR, which was recognized as a holding company for BEV operations previously under Geely Holding. In July 2021, ZEEKR established a wholly-owned subsidiary in China and acquired ZEEKR Hangzhou Bay from Geely Holding for RMB485.3 million, followed by the acquisition of ZEEKR Shanghai for RMB980.4 million in August. These transactions, treated as restructuring under common control, aimed to streamline operations and enhance ZEEKR's strategic focus on advancing electric vehicle commercialization and supporting related activities.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lotus Technology began as a business unit within Geely Auto in October 2017. Under the leadership of the co-founders, Mr. Shufu Li, Mr. Conghui An, Mr. Donghui Li, and Mr. Shengyue Gui, Lotus Technology incorporated ZEEKR Intelligent Technology as an exempted company with limited liability in March 2021 under the law of the Cayman Islands to act as its holding company. In April 2021, Zeekr Innovation, currently a wholly-owned subsidiary of ZEEKR Intelligent Technology, was incorporated under the laws of the British Virgin Islands. In the same period, Zeekr Technology, currently a wholly-owned subsidiary of Zeekr Innovation, was incorporated under the laws of Hong Kong. In April 2021, Lotus Technology announced the launch of its first BEV model, Zeekr 001, and started delivery from October 2021. Subsequently, Lotus Technology commenced deliveries of various upgraded models, including but not limited to Zeekr 001 FR in October 2023 and upgraded Zeekr 001 in February 2024. In July 2021, ZEEKR Shanghai acquired 100% equity interest in ZEEKR Hangzhou Bay from Geely Holding. In July 2021, Zhejiang ZEEKR was incorporated in the People's Republic of China and is currently a wholly-owned subsidiary of Zeekr Innovation. In August 2021, ZEEKR Innovation acquired 100% equity interest in ZEEKR Shanghai (99% from Geely Auto and 1% from Geely Holding). In October 2021, Lotus Technology acquired a 51% equity interest in Ningbo Viridi, which was previously wholly-owned by Geely Holding. In February 2022, Lotus Technology acquired 100% equity interest in Zeekr Tech EU from Geely Holding.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Corporate History 
summary: ZEEKR Intelligent Technology began as a division of Geely Auto in October 2017 and became a holding company in March 2021 under Cayman Islands law. Co-founded by key figures including Mr. Shufu Li and Mr. Conghui An, the company established several subsidiaries, including Zeekr Innovation in the British Virgin Islands and Zeekr Technology in Hong Kong. The launch of their first battery electric vehicle (BEV), the Zeekr 001, occurred in April 2021, with deliveries starting in October of the same year. The company has since introduced upgraded versions, including the Zeekr 001 FR in October 2023 and plans for another upgrade in February 2024.

In July 2021, ZEEKR Shanghai acquired ZEEKR Hangzhou Bay, and later that year, it obtained full ownership of ZEEKR Shanghai and a majority stake in Ningbo Viridi. By February 2022, ZEEKR had fully acquired Zeekr Tech EU. The company expanded its vehicle lineup with the Zeekr 009 in November 2022, followed by the luxury Zeekr 009 Grand in May 2024, and the compact SUV Zeekr X in April 2023. 

In January 2024, ZEEKR launched its first upscale sedan aimed at tech-savvy consumers. The company went public in May 2024, listing on the New York Stock Exchange under the ticker "ZK," and expanded into Southeast Asia in June 2024. The latest additions to its vehicle offerings include the Zeekr 7X, a premium electric SUV launched in September 2024, and the Zeekr MIX, which began deliveries in October 2024.

In September 2024, Lotus Technology officially unveiled and began the delivery of its premium electric five-seater SUV, the Zeekr 7X. all_retrieved_content.page_content: ZEEKR Intelligent Technology is a holding company with no operations of its own. ZEEKR Intelligent Technology conducts its business operations through its subsidiaries, including its subsidiaries in China. Under this holding company structure, investors in the American Depositary Shares (ADSs) are purchasing equity interests in the Cayman Islands holding company and obtaining indirect ownership interests in the operating companies in China. This holding company structure involves unique risks to investors, and investors may never hold equity interests in ZEEKR Intelligent Technology's operating companies in China. While ZEEKR Intelligent Technology does not operate in an industry that is currently subject to foreign ownership limitations in China, People's Republic of China (PRC) regulatory authorities could decide to limit foreign ownership in ZEEKR Intelligent Technology's industry in the future, in which case there could be a risk that ZEEKR Intelligent Technology would be unable to do business in China as currently structured. If the PRC government deems that any of ZEEKR Intelligent Technology's business operations carried out by its Hong Kong or PRC subsidiaries were to be restricted or prohibited from foreign investment in the future, ZEEKR Intelligent Technology may be required to stop its business operations in China, and ZEEKR Intelligent Technology could be subject to material penalties or be forced to relinquish its interests in the affected operations. Such events could result in a material change in ZEEKR Intelligent Technology's operations and a material change in the value of its securities, including causing the value of such securities to significantly decline or become worthless.

title: Holding Company Structure 
summary: ZEEKR Intelligent Technology operates as a holding company primarily in China, with its investors acquiring equity interests through American Depositary Shares (ADSs). This structure creates indirect ownership of Chinese operating companies, exposing investors to unique risks, particularly regarding potential future restrictions on foreign investment imposed by the Chinese government. Such restrictions could severely impact ZEEKR's operations, possibly leading to significant penalties or the cessation of its business in China, which would adversely affect the value of its securities.

The company also faces considerable legal and operational challenges due to the complex and evolving regulatory landscape in China. Key risks include difficulties in obtaining regulatory approvals for overseas offerings and foreign investments, increased scrutiny on cybersecurity and data privacy, and potential anti-monopoly actions. These factors could hinder ZEEKR's ability to offer securities and may lead to a substantial decline in the value of its ADSs.

Currently, ZEEKR has not declared or paid any dividends and has no immediate plans to do so. Its ability to pay dividends in the future will depend on profits from its subsidiaries, which face restrictions under PRC laws regarding dividend payments and asset transfers. Dividends can only be distributed from net profits after tax, and subsidiaries must allocate a portion of profits to statutory reserves. Additionally, any dividends paid to non-PRC-resident enterprises are subject to withholding taxes, and remitting dividends abroad involves compliance with specific foreign exchange and tax regulations. Future regulatory changes may further restrict foreign currency access, complicating the company's ability to meet dividend obligations to offshore entities.

The Group has not recognized any significant unrecognized tax benefits or related penalties during the reviewed periods, indicating a stable outlook regarding tax compliance and potential liabilities.
